other_observers: Brandon Percival; Brandon Percival found this bird on 18 January.
description: This was a long-billed warbler. It was overall dull greenish with a hint of yellow at the throat and chin. It was relatively short-tailed, with dark checks, and the wings, with two dull whitish wingbars, contrasted little with the body color.Behaviors: Fed in a sluggish manner, exploring both deciduous and conifer needles. Fed on "sap" oozing from a sliver maple, and attempted to pick up a dark insect from the top side of a small maple twig. Explored upside down, right side up and twisted in all manners while exploring. Checked twigs, leaf buds, broken parts of twigs for food itemes. Sometimes silent and sometimes vocal chips from tallest parts of trees. Often foraged alone.Call: loud chips given on occassion, distinct from those of Myrtle and Audubon WarblersPlumage: 1st winter
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
